source_paper_title: Boole’s Probability Bounding Problem, Linear Programming Aggregations, and Nonnegative Quadratic Pseudo-Boolean Functions
context:
Page 22, Section 6 (Conclusions), Remark 3

"Based on this we conjecture that these two bounds are in fact identical."
title: Equality of the IPG and DeCaen lower bounds
latex:
Given an integer \(n\ge 2\), let \(A_1,\dots,A_n\) be events on a common probability space with given singleton probabilities \(p_i = \mathbb{P}(A_i)\) for \(i\in[n]\) and pairwise intersection probabilities \(p_{ij}=\mathbb{P}(A_i\cap A_j)\) for all \(1\le i<j\le n\). Consider Boole’s union-bounding problem for \(m=2\): determine the minimum possible value of \(\mathbb{P}(\bigcup_{i=1}^n A_i)\) over all such event systems consistent with \((p_i,p_{ij})\).

Define the Hailperin LP relaxation optimum \(\mathrm{LB}(H)\) as the minimum value of Hailperin’s linear program (equivalently (3) in the paper).

Define \(\mathrm{LB}(\mathrm{IPG})\) as the optimum of the strengthened Pr\'ekopa–Gao aggregation (15) (the PG model plus the full cone constraints \(U_1\)).

Define the DeCaen lower bound \(\mathrm{LB}(\mathrm{Dec})\) as the closed-form lower bound on \(\mathbb{P}(\bigcup_{i=1}^n A_i)\) given by DeCaen (1997), computed from \((p_i,p_{ij})\).

Problem: prove or refute that for every \(n\) and every feasible input \((p_i,p_{ij})\),
\[\mathrm{LB}(\mathrm{IPG}) = \mathrm{LB}(\mathrm{Dec}).\]
Equivalently, determine whether the LP lower bound produced by the strengthened Pr\'ekopa–Gao aggregation always coincides with DeCaen’s bound, or characterize precisely when they differ.
open_problem_quote: "Based on this we conjecture that these two bounds are in fact identical."

verification.notice: This entry was generated from the paper text only. It has not been independently verified as still open. If you believe the question is solved or incorrectly stated, please contact the maintainers.
